data.title: RPG Fun, LLC's AI Role Playing Game platform receives U.S. Patent No. 12,330,066 for Just-In-Time Game Engine.
data.description: A tech company based in Menlo Park, California, recently received a U.S. Patent (No. 12,330,066) from the United States Patent and Trademark Office. RPG Fun, LLC, the company known for their AI Role Playing Game platform called AIRPGHUB, is credited with inventing the "Just-In-Time Game Engine for Game World."

data.title: TechnipFMC (NYSE: FTI) reports Q2 earnings of 68 cents/share and sales of $2.53B, beating estimates. Affirms FY2025 sales guidance of $9.6B-$10.15B. CEO Doug Pferdehirt proud of team's strong performance.
data.description: TechnipFMC plc (NYSE: FTI) announced strong second-quarter results, surpassing expectations with an EPS of 68 cents compared to the estimated 58 cents. The company's quarterly sales also exceeded predictions, reaching $2.53 billion against the projected $2.48 billion. Despite this success, TechnipFMC maintained its FY2025 sales forecast between $9.60 billion and $10.15 billion. Doug Pferdehirt, the Chair and CEO of TechnipFMC, commended the team for achieving another robust quarter, attributing the performance to the team's dedicated execution in both commercial and operational aspects.

data.title: Sharplink responds to BitMine's $2B Ether purchase with hiring of BlackRock veteran after rival's big buy.
data.description: A 20-year BlackRock veteran has been hired by SharpLink, a company that made headlines the day after its competitor BitMine revealed a significant purchase of Ether.

data.title: James S. Bostwick named to 2025 Northern California Super Lawyers list. San Francisco, July 25, 2025 - PRNewswire.
data.description: James S. Bostwick, the Founding Partner of Bostwick & Associates, has been chosen to be featured in the upcoming 2025 edition of Northern California Super Lawyers. This announcement was made on July 25, 2025, in San Francisco. James S. Bostwick has been recognized for his outstanding legal expertise and has been included in the prestigious list of Northern California Super Lawyers.

data.title: South Korea prefabricated construction market to grow at 4% CAGR, reaching KRW 15.90 trillion by 2029 - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: A report on the South Korea Prefabricated Construction Market indicates promising growth trends. The market is forecasted to grow steadily at a rate of 4.7% annually, with expectations to reach KRW 13.00 trillion by 2025. The report, titled "South Korea Prefabricated Construction Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics Databook," offers insights on over 100 key performance indicators (KPIs), market size, and forecasts based on end markets, precast products, and precast materials. This data update was made in Q2 of 2025, showcasing the market's robust trajectory.

data.title: InventHelp inventor from Amagansett, N.Y. develops COPLIN HAIR GROWTH formula to stimulate and strengthen hair.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Pittsburgh, an inventor from Amagansett, N.Y., shared their motivation behind creating the COPLIN HAIR GROWTH product. They aimed to offer consumers a fast and straightforward method to enhance hair growth. The innovator highlighted that their invention is designed to stimulate and strengthen hair effectively. The COPLIN HAIR GROWTH product promises to address these concerns efficiently.

data.title: Global rail asset management market projected to reach $17.59 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 6.6%.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, recent data from MarketsandMarkets™ reveals a projected increase in the global Rail Asset Management Market. The market size is anticipated to rise from USD 12.79 billion in 2025 to USD 17.59 billion by 2030, indicating a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.6% throughout the forecast period.

data.title: VAPORESSO launches the iMate OS vaping product at CHAMPS 2025 in Las Vegas, marking brand's 10th anniversary.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Las Vegas, VAPORESSO introduced the iMate OS at the CHAMPS Trade Summer Show 2025, commemorating their 10th anniversary. The iMate OS is the industry's inaugural modular vaping product compatible with all categories. This new product aims to innovate in the vaping industry and was showcased at the event, highlighting VAPORESSO's commitment to advancement.

data.title: NHPF breaks ground on Galena Apartments, a 54-unit workforce housing project in Frisco, CO, their first project in Colorado.
data.description: NHPF, a national provider focused on quality affordable housing, recently commenced their initial project in Colorado with the groundbreaking of Galena Apartments. This new development in Frisco comprises 54 units specifically designed for workforce housing, boasting an all-electric setup. The commencement of this project signifies a significant step for NHPF into the Colorado market.

data.title: US stocks higher, Nasdaq up 0.2%; Dow at 44,717.61 (+0.05%) and S&P 500 at 6,374.18 (+0.17%). Charter Communications shares plunge 15% after Q2 with revenue at $13.77B.
data.description: U.S. stocks opened higher today, with the Nasdaq Composite increasing by 0.2% on Friday. The Dow rose by 0.05% to 44,717.61, while the NASDAQ climbed 0.20% to 21,099.18. The S&P 500 also saw gains, rising by 0.17% to 6,374.18. Consumer discretionary shares saw a 0.4% jump, while real estate stocks fell by 0.7% on Thursday. Charter Communications (NASDAQ: CHTR) stock fell approximately 15% after reporting its Q2 2025 results, showing a 0.6% revenue growth to $13.77 billion, surpassing estimates. However, earnings per share of $9.18 missed the estimated $9.80. Linkhome Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: ) was among the equities trading up.

data.title: HCA Healthcare exceeds Q2 revenue expectations with $18.61 billion, aiming for $76 billion revenue total. Robust earnings growth.
data.description: HCA Healthcare Inc., a leading hospital operator traded on NYSE under HCA, exceeded analyst expectations in the second quarter of 2025, showcasing strength in the healthcare industry despite a slight decline in patient numbers. The company reported total revenues of $18.61 billion for the quarter, marking a 6.4% rise from the previous year and slightly beating the anticipated $18.49 billion. Their adjusted earnings per share stood at $6.84, a significant increase from $5.50 in the previous year and exceeding the projected $6.24. In the second quarter of 2025, there was a 1.8% increase in same-facility admissions, demonstrating positive performance. This information was disclosed in a detailed report available for further reading on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Carter's, Inc. (CRI) shares drop due to weak Q2 earnings and tariff concerns. Sales at $585.31M (+4% YoY) missed EPS estimate. CEO disappointed.
data.description: Shares of Carter’s, Inc. (NYSE: CRI) experienced a decline as weak earnings and increasing tariff concerns dimmed the retailer's future prospects. In the second quarter, the company reported adjusted earnings per share of 17 cents, falling short of the analyst consensus of 34 cents. Despite this, quarterly sales stood at $585.31 million, marking a 4% increase from the previous year and surpassing the Street's expectation of $561.14 million. However, adjusted operating income for the quarter plummeted to $11.8 million, a significant drop from $39.5 million in the same period last year. Carter's CEO & president, Douglas C. Palladini, expressed disappointment over the decline in profitability, citing factors such as strategic investments in pricing, new stores, and returning to normal performance levels. For more details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
